name: Operations/ms-reindex
description: $reindex operation conformance tests: async job kickoff, job listing, job-status polling, and synchronous per-resource reindex (from ReindexTests). $reindex is a Microsoft-specific operation, commonly disabled or absent on other servers, so status assertions are warningOnly.

variable.name: reindexJobLocation
variable.description: Job-status URL from the kickoff response's Content-Location header; falls back to GET $reindex (list jobs) if the header is absent.

variable.name: reindexTargetPatientId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Patient created for the per-resource $reindex test

operation.description: Create a synthetic Patient to use as the target of the per-resource $reindex test

test.name: $reindex kickoff returns 201 Created with a Content-Location job-status header
test.description: POST $reindex with no parameters must return 201 Created with a Content-Location header pointing to the job status. warningOnly: $reindex may be disabled or unimplemented.

assert.description: Kickoff should return 201 Created (warningOnly: $reindex may be disabled or unimplemented on this target)
Response body should be a Parameters resource describing the new job (warningOnly)
Response should include a Content-Location header with the job status URL (warningOnly)

test.name: $reindex kickoff accepts maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery / maximumNumberOfResourcesPerWrite tuning parameters
test.description: POST $reindex with maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery and maximumNumberOfResourcesPerWrite tuning parameters must also return 201 Created. warningOnly: same environment dependency as the basic kickoff test.

assert.description: Kickoff with tuning parameters should return 201 Created (warningOnly)

test.name: GET $reindex lists any active reindex jobs
test.description: GET $reindex must return 200 with a Parameters resource listing active reindex jobs. warningOnly: $reindex may be disabled or unimplemented.

assert.description: Response body should be a Parameters resource (warningOnly)

test.name: $reindex with an invalid Prefer header value is rejected with 400 Bad Request
test.description: POST $reindex with an unsupported Prefer header value must return 400 Bad Request. warningOnly: header validation is server-specific and $reindex may be unimplemented.

operation.description: POST $reindex with an unsupported Prefer header value

assert.description: Server should reject the unsupported Prefer header with 400 Bad Request (warningOnly)

test.name: $reindex with a non-numeric maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery is rejected with 400 Bad Request
test.description: POST $reindex with a non-numeric maximumNumberOfResourcesPerQuery must return 400 Bad Request. warningOnly: some servers may coerce or ignore invalid tuning parameters instead of rejecting them.

assert.description: Server should reject the non-numeric parameter with 400 Bad Request (warningOnly)

test.name: Reindex job status can be polled via the Content-Location URL
test.description: GET the job-status URL from the kickoff's Content-Location header must return 200 with a Parameters resource containing a 'status' parameter. warningOnly: depends on the kickoff test having returned a Content-Location header.

operation.description: GET the reindex job status URL

assert.description: Response should be a Parameters resource with a 'status' parameter (warningOnly)

test.name: Per-resource $reindex synchronously reindexes a single resource
test.description: POST Patient/{id}/$reindex must synchronously reindex the resource and return 200 with a Parameters result. warningOnly: $reindex may be disabled or unimplemented.

assert.description: Response body should be a Parameters resource (warningOnly)

operation.description: Teardown: delete the Patient created for the per-resource $reindex test
